methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C autonomous transportation company founded by Raquel Urtasun, has raised $1.0B to date. The broader Transportation sector includes seven identified private peers (Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft, and two others) and no public comparables, limiting the depth of peer-based valuation anchors.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using $1.0B in total capital raised as a proxy for current value, a typical Series C-to-later-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x yields an implied valuation of $1.7B. A peer-median approach was not viable; while five Transportation peers exist, only two fell within the narrow same-stage filter, and zero appeared in adjacent-stage or similar-valuation-tier cohorts—below the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B (median $1.7B), with $1.7B as the point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage peer transaction data, which limits calibration of the stage-progression multiplier for this specific sector. The single-method foundation also constrains confidence in the range. Additional data on recent Transportation Series C+ financings or exit multiples would materially sharpen this estimate.
